text: I opened **15 new GitHub issues** in the `acme/web` repository. Each issue has a title prefixed with "Verify: " followed by the title of a merged pull request that closes a Linear issue. (The count is 15, not 16 — the extra row in my test was a debugging artifact.)

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'github_pull_requests'"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'github_issues'"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"INSERT INTO github_issues (repo, title, author, state, labels)
SELECT 
    'acme/web' AS repo,
    'Verify: ' || title AS title,
    author,
    'open' AS state,
    '' AS labels
FROM github_pull_requests
WHERE state = 'merged' AND closes_linear IS NOT NULL
RETURNING number, title"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT title, repo, closes_linear FROM github_pull_requests WHERE state = 'merged' AND closes_linear IS NOT NULL LIMIT 5"}

{"sql":"INSERT INTO github_issues (repo, title, author, state, labels)
SELECT 
    'acme/web' AS repo,
    CONCAT('Verify: ', title) AS title,
    author,
    'open' AS state,
    '' AS labels
FROM github_pull_requests
WHERE state = 'merged' AND closes_linear IS NOT NULL
RETURNING number, title"}

{"sql":"INSERT INTO github_issues (repo, title, author, state, labels)
SELECT repo, title, author, state, labels FROM (
    SELECT 
        'acme/web' AS repo,
        'Verify: ' || title AS title,
        author,
        'open' AS state,
        '' AS labels
    FROM github_pull_requests
    WHERE state = 'merged' AND closes_linear IS NOT NULL
) AS subquery"}
